spec_match.rationale: Uses document.activeViewTransition as a real concurrent guard, wraps each call in performance.mark/measure, and surfaces the live ViewTransition object — all spec-direct usage.

interactive.rationale: Tile grid of 8 view-transition-named elements, stress-test (5 rapid) button, clear button, live stat row of 6 measurements, and an event log — well above the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb promises performance.measure + PerformanceObserver + a 5-call stress test, and the page implements all three.

edge_cases.rationale: Guard-skip path is exercised by the stress test and an unsupported-browser banner is shown. Missing: a paint entry buffered before the observer attaches (the demo registers the observer with buffered:true but doesn't surface that), tile-without-a-view-transition-name case, and what happens if performance.mark itself throws (rare but documented in the spec).

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5067126381215744 and MDN, but omits the css-view-transitions-2 spec anchor that the sibling concepts include.

design_system.rationale: Most styles use CSS variables, but .btn-stress sets background: var(--accent-rose) with color: var(--bg-ivory) (passes WCAG AA) — and on hover it inverts to bg-paper / accent-rose. The hover invert is fine; double-check the inverted state on actual paper background. Otherwise design system compliant.

openQuestions.title: Add the spec link to the references list
openQuestions.detail: The sibling concepts (active-vt, concurrent-guard, external-skip) all link to https://drafts.csswg.org/css-view-transitions-2/#dom-document-activeviewtransition. This page links MDN but not the spec — add the spec anchor for parity and so the per-concept reviewer rubric's references row clears cleanly.

openQuestions.title: Surface paint entries by name, not just a counter
openQuestions.detail: The PerformanceObserver buffers paint entries but only increments a counter. Authors debugging transition jank want to know WHICH paint entry — first-paint vs first-contentful-paint vs paint entries from within startViewTransition's snapshot. Add a small table that lists the most recent 3-5 entries with their name and startTime so the observer's value is visible.

openQuestions.title: Compare guarded vs unguarded stress run
openQuestions.detail: The stress test currently always uses the guard, so the demo of the guard's value is implicit. Add an 'unguarded mode' toggle that runs the 5-call burst WITHOUT the activeViewTransition check, so authors see startViewTransition's behaviour under overlap (AbortErrors, dropped frames) vs the guarded version side-by-side.

summary: The most ambitious of the four concepts — real performance instrumentation, a live PerformanceObserver, and a stress mode. Two follow-ups would lift it to gold: surface individual paint entries by name, and add a guarded/unguarded comparison so the guard's value is shown not just claimed.
